queue.ask: Research and design a beautiful 2D parallax exterior for the sim interior. The agent should: (1) Study how top-tier 2D Godot games do parallax city/sky backgrounds — reference Celeste, Owlboy, Dead Cells for layering techniques. (2) Review the browser prototype's exterior (Three.js skyline, altitude-based sky color, stars at high altitude, city at ground level). (3) Propose a multi-layer parallax system: deep sky/stars layer (0.1x scroll), mid clouds/atmosphere (0.3x), city skyline silhouette (0.5x), near buildings (0.7x), foreground details (0.9x). (4) Design altitude-aware visuals: ground level sees city, mid-tower sees horizon, top floors see stars. (5) Write a implementation brief with exact Godot node structure, shaders if needed, and color palettes per altitude band.
queue.transfer_value: Sets the visual bar for the entire game. The exterior through the windows is the player's constant companion — it must be beautiful and reward vertical progress.
subtasks: Research parallax techniques in acclaimed 2D Godot games
Study Godot ParallaxBackground/ParallaxLayer nodes vs manual scroll
Design 5-layer depth system with scroll ratios
Create altitude-aware color palette (ground=warm amber, mid=blue, top=deep indigo/stars)
Propose shader approach for sky gradient that shifts with camera Y
Write implementation brief for Claude Code to execute

queue.ask: What scene tree structure do you want for the sim interior? 10 floors × 12 blocks × modules × NPCs × collision.
